name: Snow Rollers

distinctive_features: hollow center resembling a doughnut or Swiss roll
visual_attributes.texture: smooth outer surface with potential roughness from snow accumulation
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: ranging from the size of a tennis ball to larger than a car
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: concentric layers with a hollow core

domain_specific_visual.formation_process: formed by rolling motion due to wind or gravity
domain_specific_visual.environmental_conditions: requires specific snow and wind conditions

domain_specific_functional.meteorological_significance: indicates presence of specific snow and wind conditions

relationships.description: Snow rollers require a thin surface layer of wet, loose snow to form.
Snow rollers need a substrate like ice or powder snow that the surface layer will not stick to.
Wind strong enough to move but not destroy the snow rollers.
Gravity can move snow rollers down a hill.

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: hilly areas with specific snow conditions
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: rare and visually intriguing natural phenomenon

contrastive_constraints: NOT common snow formations like snowdrifts
